free_text: -Integrated data from measurement of absolute
differential neutron emission cross-section at 14 MeV
incident neutron energy.-
Complete revision of all data
Only graphs,for Ti,V and Cr
Only graphs for Mn,Fe,Co,Ni,Cu,Zn
Only graphs for Na,Mg,Al,Si,P and S
Only graphs for Ga,Se,Br,Zr,Nb,Cd,
In,Sn,Sb,J,Ta,W,Au,Hg,Pb and Bi
150 KV-cascade accelerator with pulsing system
Pulsed deuteron beam on thick T-target
Incident neutron energy depends on the scattering angle
in the range from 14.5 to 14.75 MeV.  The average
energy is ''14.6 MeV''.
Time-of-flight with an overall time-resolution
of 3 nanoseconds.
Liquid scintillator  Ne-213
Neutrons
For details of the measuring technique see report
ZFK - 277(U)/INDC(GDR)-2/l  and EXFOR 30275.001
Neutron source strength was absolutely determined by
the associated particle method
Integration over double differential neutron emission
cross-sections (EXFOR 30275.) by expansion in terms
of a series of legendre polynomials
Errors are mainly due to the integration
procedure (expansion of the angular distributions in
terms of Legendre-polynomials)
Dependent data on exfor 30275. and above
mentioned integration procedure
Approved by the author,77/05/19.
Data compiled by authors
Authors revision of all data by unique appli
cation of Legendre polynomial fit program
-OS.-  Implementation of authors' 1982
revision. All data sections replaced by revised
tables as received on tape from authors.
TMK. Ref. moved from BIB section to proper
subentries
BIB section updated
